mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates applied to future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns following months when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC > 0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0% from a lower level, it signals weakening aggregate demand and rising corporate cost-cutting, triggering de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices measured by CPIAUCSL combined with a falling federal funds rate generate negative real policy rates that stimulate nominal corporate revenues faster than discount rates rise, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns; this signal requires both series to move jointly.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when the economy is in a monetary tightening cycle; outside of tightening, the signal is null because demand weakness is offset by rate cuts.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) following RSAFS declines during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the signal works equally well outside tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding corporate output and operating leverage, leading to positive earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months when INDPRO rises, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP (GDPC1) below the prior quarter's level signal contraction in aggregate output and corporate revenue downturns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with an unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that constrains corporate margins through wage pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances when both UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS is rising, or the interaction term does not improve prediction versus UNRATE alone (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction and durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months when HOUST is below 1200, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
